NVDA.reasoning:
MACRO REGIME: Transitional risk-on. S&P 500 hit a new all-time high on April 30 (+0.8%), April tracking as best month since 2020 (+9% MTD). Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% (third consecutive hold, unusual 8-4 dissent) — higher-for-longer baseline but no hike threat. AI capex supercycle intact: Alphabet and Amazon confirmed Q1 expansion. Meta's -9% capex shock is the primary NVDA-specific risk if it spreads to other hyperscalers. Iranian blockade (Strait of Hormuz) is the dominant macro left-tail. Net regime read: risk-on with meaningful tail risks — wind at the back but gusts possible.

RELATIVE STRENGTH: NVDA is outperforming on the 21-day timeframe (+14.43% ROC) but showing sharp short-term weakness (5-day ROC -0.04%, hourly 10d ROC -4.73%). Price dropped ~8% intraday from the $216.83 recent high to $199.57, closing below EMA-5, EMA-10, and short-term hourly MAs, while still holding above EMA-20 ($198.14), EMA-50 ($190.52), and EMA-200 ($177.38). Daily EMA alignment remains bullish. The daily trend structure is intact — this reads as a pullback within an uptrend, not a breakdown, particularly given ADX at 55.96 with Plus DI (29.83) >> Minus DI (14.49).

KEY SIGNAL: Hourly RSI at 23.58 (deeply oversold) with OBV trending UP while price declines = bullish divergence. The $199 support zone has been tested 18 times on the hourly chart. Prev-day hourly close at $200.82 — price is probing but holding near this zone. Daily RSI-14 at 59.74 and RSI-21 at 67.88 are NOT stretched (prior stop-hit lesson cited RSI 81-87 on entry — this is a different setup at a far more reasonable RSI level).

ASYMMETRY: Entry near $199.57 (current support zone). Stop below recent low at $194.00 (2.8% downside, below $198.70 intraday low with buffer). Target at $214.73 (hourly pivot resistance, near prior high cluster): 7.6% upside. R/R = 7.6% / 2.8% = 2.7:1. Meets the 2:1 minimum, approaches the preferred 3:1 threshold.

CAPITAL PRESERVATION: NVDA balance sheet is not a concern — near-zero leverage, 59% operating margins, 61% revenue growth. ATR at $6.27 (3.14%) is consistent with the AI-sector volatility regime. The primary risk is thesis-level: if a second hyperscaler (Amazon or MSFT) revises AI capex lower, NVDA demand narrative is directly impacted. Manage this via the stop, not avoidance. Lessons check: prior stop-hit on 2026-04-30 was from an RSI-stretched entry (81-87 daily); current entry is at RSI-14 = 59.74 with hourly deeply oversold — materially different risk profile. Confidence capped at 62 to reflect: (1) one recent loss on the same ticker, (2) Meta capex contagion risk unresolved, (3) transitional macro with Iran tail.
